source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The Bode plot of a transfer function \( G(s) \) is shown in the <image>. The gain \( 20 \log |G(s)| \) is 32 dB and -8 dB at 1 rad/s and 10 rad/s respectively. The phase is negative for all \( \omega \). Then \( G(s) \) is:

answer letter B was mapped to the corresponding choice text

image_dependency: question explicitly says the Bode plot is shown in the figure
determining G(s) likely depends on visual slope/shape information from the plot, not only the quoted gain values
multi_step: requires combining magnitude information at 1 rad/s and 10 rad/s to infer slope across one decade
requires mapping inferred slope to system order/pole structure and using phase-negative condition to disambiguate the form
multiple-choice selection among 4 transfer functions adds a comparison step
verifiability: answer is a specific closed-form transfer function with fixed numeric gain
the choice can be checked from Bode magnitude change (32 dB to -8 dB over one decade) and phase sign condition

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present with no registry issues
high image dependency makes this a valuable multimodal sample
multi-step reasoning and high verifiability support strong collection value
